headlines.title: Secretary Hegseth and Gen. Caine testify at House hearing
headlines.description: Watch live coverage as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman Gen. Dan Caine testify at a House Armed Services Committee hearing. The two are expected to face questions on the ongoing war with Iran and the Pentagon's budget request for the upcoming year.

headlines.title: US Gas Prices Surge to a 4-Year High as Iran Stalemate Drags On
headlines.description: Gas prices surged to their highest levels in nearly four years as negotiations to end the war between the United States and Iran remain deadlocked. The average cost for a gallon of regular now stands at $4.23 and experts warn the timing couldn’t be worse with the summer travel season just weeks away. NBC’s Christine Romans reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Hegseth testifies before House over Pentagon's budget amid ongoing Iran war
headlines.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before the House of Representatives for the first time since the Iran war began to answer questions about the military's $1.5 trillion budget request.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rules 2024 Louisiana redistricting map is unconstitutional
headlines.description: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court ruled that a congressional map in Louisiana was a racial gerrymander even though it was drawn to comply with the landmark Voting Rights Act, which is aimed at protecting minority voters. NBC News' Ryan Reilly and Julie Tsirkin report on how this could make it harder for civil rights plaintiffs to challenge future maps.

headlines.title: Highlights From King Charles' State Dinner, Address to Congress
headlines.description: President Donald Trump and the first lady hosted King Charles III and Queen Camilla at a lavish state dinner where the King acknowledged the recent assassination attempt at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner. Charles later addressed a joint meeting of Congress, reaffirming US-UK relations and emphasizing the importance of NATO. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: New pictures emerge of suspect in correspondents' dinner shooting
headlines.description: The California man accused of opening fire outside the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner snapped a selfie before the attack, donned in black with ammunition strapped to his body, newly released images revealed Wednesday

headlines.title: First Amendment advocates blast the FCC's early review of ABC broadcast licenses
headlines.description: Free speech advocates sounded the alarm Tuesday over the Federal Communications Commission’s challenge to ABC’s broadcast licenses.

headlines.title: White House ramps up pressure on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel over first lady remark
headlines.description: The White House on Tuesday intensified pressure on ABC to fire late-night host Jimmy Kimmel over a comment he made about first lady Melania Trump last week.

headlines.title: Stop scrolling and eat lentils: New guidelines to prevent hemorrhoids
headlines.description: The American Gastroenterological Association released new guidelines to prevent hemorrhoids and the constipation that causes them.

headlines.title: Interest rates expected to hold steady at Fed meeting likely to be Powell's last as chair
headlines.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates steady at what is likely to be Jerome Powell’s last policy meeting as chair.

headlines.title: Pentagon says Iran war has cost $25 billion so far
headlines.description: Pentagon says Iran war has cost $25 billion so far

headlines.title: Hegseth criticizes 'defeatist' comments about Iran war
headlines.description: Hegseth criticizes 'defeatist' comments about Iran war
